characters.name: Alverson
characters.description: Alversonis a Chicago alderman. He is a prominent figure in the black caucus but usually defers to AldermanRoss. He is tapped by MayorTom Kaneto replace Ross as the leader of the black caucus when Ross withdraws from politics because of his loyalty. However, he is blackmailed into voting against Kane by Ross's aideMona Fredericks; she threatens to put "crazy shit" that he has written on the internet.[1] Alverson is a recurring character in thefirstandsecondseasons. He is played by co-starPhillip Edward Van Lear.

characters.name: Angry Inmate
characters.description: AnAngry Inmateis incarcerated withEmma Kane. She threatensEmma Kaneafter she tries to get her to change the television channel. She is warned off by aLarge Inmate.[1] An Angry Inmate is an unnamed minor character in thesecondseason. She is played by co-starElizabeth Ledoand only appears in "Louder Than Words".

characters.name: Gwendolyn Archer
characters.description: Gwendolyn Archeris an aide to SenatorCatherine Walsh. She books Walsh on a tour of moderate sized cities throughout Illinois so she can campaign for governor. Walsh criticizes the strategy as lacking ambition, believing that she already has the support of her base and needs to focus on the populous of Chicago. She cancels the tour to campaign in Chicago itself.[1] Gwendolyn Archer is a recurring character in thefirstandsecondseasons. She is played by co-starKayte Grammer.

She cancels the tour to campaign in Chicago itself.[1] Gwendolyn Archer is a recurring character in thefirstandsecondseasons. She is played by co-starKayte Grammer.

characters.name: Peter Baine
characters.description: Peter Baineis a media entrepreneur and the owner of the Chicago Sentinel newspaper. He forces Miller to apologize after an editorial he writes offends corporation that advertise with his company.[1] Peter Baine is a recurring character in thefirstandsecondseasons. He is played by co-starCraig Spidle.

characters.name: Mike Bosovic
characters.description: Mike Bosovicis the Chief Executive Officer of Scientia. His company is involved in the improvement of Inner City Schools, using funding from the Chicago City Council.Meredith Kaneis in charge of both funding and publicizing the project. A Scientia publicity event goes awry when journalists notice concealed mold at a supposedly much improved inner city school. Meredith warns Bosovic to get his act together because the bad publicity reflects poorly on her and the mayor.[1] Mike Bosovic is a recurring character in thefirstandsecondseasons. He is played by co-starMichael Accardo.

characters.name: Chief Exhumer
characters.description: AChief Exhumeris working on the O'Hare cemetery relocation project. He gives a speech to his men and the construction workers, explaining that the construction workers need to remove the top soil before the exhumers can get to work.[1] A Chief Exhumer is an unnamed minor character in thefirstseason. He is played by co-starLoren Lazerineand only appears in "Listen".

characters.name: Chuck
characters.description: Chuckis a city desk reporter for the Chicago Sentinel. He congratulatesSam Milleron an editorial he has written about political corruption. He commiserates with Miller when he is forced to apologize to some of the advertisers offended by the police by the papers ownership.[1] Chuck is a recurring character in thefirstandsecondseasons. He is played by co-starBrian King.

characters.name: Clerk
characters.description: AClerkserves the Chicago City Council. She collates the votes on MayorTom Kane's ordnance to close the Lennox Gardens housing project. The proceedings are interrupted by AldermanOrtizfighting with a colleague and Kane suspends the vote.[1] A Clerk is an unnamed minor character in thesecondseason. She is played by co-starCheryl Hamadaand only appears in "Louder Than Words".

characters.name: Clinic Doctor
characters.description: AClinic Doctorworks at the free clinic withEmma Kane. He treatsAlton Morrisonafter Emma books him into the clinic.[1] A Clinic Doctor is an unnamed minor character in thefirstseason. He is played by co-starDale Riveraand only appears in "Listen".

characters.name: Clinic Volunteer
characters.description: AClinic Volunteerworks at the church free clinic withEmma Kane. She takes a call from MayorTom Kaneand passes the message on to Emma.[1] A Clinic Volunteer is an unnamed minor character in thefirstseason. She is played by co-starMia Parkand only appears in "Listen".

characters.name: McCall Cullen
characters.description: McCall Cullen, often called Mac, is the incumbent Governor of Illinois. He is serving his fourth and final term, having lost his party's nomination to run for re-election to State TreasurerBen Zajac. Mayor of ChicagoTom Kanespeaks in support of Governor Cullen for re-election at a campaign event. Kane then backs away from his crony to support young and ambitious state treasurerBen Zajacto replace Cullen. He takes Zajac to the City Hall roof gardens, ensuring that their meeting is publicized and sending a message to Cullen.[1] Zajac calls him for advice about campaigning against rival gubernatorial nominee SenatorCatherine Walsh, who Mac has defeated in the past. Mac relishes Zajac's apology for the primary and then tells him to "eat shit."[2]

characters.name: Linda Driscoll
characters.description: Linda Driscollis a Chicago Alderman. She employedIan Toddas a key aide but has loaned him to MayorTom Kaneto fill the gap in his staff left by the death ofEzra Stoneand the departure ofKitty O'Neill. She supports Kane's ordnance to close the Lennoz Gardens housing project despite reservations about the lack of a relocation plan for the displaced residents.[1] It is revealed in episode 3 that Ian Todd is Alderman Driscolls lover, despite the fact she appears to be married with a family.

characters.name: Billy English
characters.description: Billy Englishis the chief of an Illinois Indian tribe who take an interest in the preservation of historical paintings found at the O'Hare Airport redevelopment site. Kane meets with English and his people and pledges to preserve the find in a museum.[1] Billy English is a recurring character in thefirst season. He is played by co-starJoseph Fleetfoot Sada.

characters.name: Field Reporter
characters.description: AField ReporterinterviewsMeredith Kanefor television at the O'Hare Airport redevelopment groundbreaking ceremony. She questions Meredith about the redevelopment project.[1] A Field Reporter is an unnamed minor character in thesecondseason. She is played by co-starNancy Penderand only appears in "Louder Than Words".

characters.name: Foreman
characters.description: AForemanis supervising building work at the O'Hare redevelopment. He is overseeing the relocation of a cemetery that is in the way of the O'Hare redevelopment.[1] A Foreman is an unnamed minor character in thesecondseason. He is played by co-starRoy Andersonand only appears in "Listen".

characters.name: Foreman
characters.description: AForemanis supervising building work at the O'Hare redevelopment. He is overseeing the relocation of a cemetery that is in the way of the O'Hare redevelopment.[1] A Foreman is an unnamed minor character in thesecondseason. He is played by co-starRoy Andersonand only appears in "Listen".

characters.name: Mona Fredericks
characters.description: Mona Fredericksis the senior aide to Alderman Ross. Mona is Alderman Ross’ strong-willed and politically savvy Chief of Staff. A native of Chicago’s notorious South Side, Mona grew up in the projects and pursued a career in politics to help the marginalized community from which she came. Incorruptible and unwavering in her ethics, she is every bit as dedicated to her husband and children as she is to her job. Impressed with her aptitude and intrigued by her integrity, Kane takes an interest in Mona that perhaps goes a step beyond political admiration.[1] Mona Fredericks is a major character in thesecond season. She is played by starring cast memberSanaa Lathan.

She is played by starring cast memberSanaa Lathan.

characters.name: Garza
characters.description: Garzais a construction worker on the O'Hare Airport redevelopment project. The construction crew are assigned to help to relocate a graveyard that is in the path of the project, exhuming bodies to be moved to a new site. Garza discovers ancient Native American paintings in a grave.[1] Garza is a recurring character in thefirst season. He is played by co-starSal Velez.

characters.name: Grey Haired Man
characters.description: Grey Haired Manis a criminal enforcer servicing the needs of corrupt politicians via Mayoral Chief of StaffEzra Stone. He assaults Dr.Ella Harrisin her car as she is picking up her son from a baseball game. He injects her with a neuromuscular blocking agent, temporarily paralyzing her. He warns her not to break the confidentiality of MayorTom Kane.[1]

characters.name: Grey Haired Man
characters.description: Grey Haired Manis a criminal enforcer servicing the needs of corrupt politicians via Mayoral Chief of StaffEzra Stone. He assaults Dr.Ella Harrisin her car as she is picking up her son from a baseball game. He injects her with a neuromuscular blocking agent, temporarily paralyzing her. He warns her not to break the confidentiality of MayorTom Kane.[1]

characters.name: Ella Harris
characters.description: Ella Harrisis a neurologist. She is treating MayorTom Kanefor Lewy Body Dementia. Dr. Harris sees Mayor of ChicagoTom Kanein secrecy, meeting him at a disused abattoir. She diagnoses him with Lewy Body Dementia, a progressive degenerative disease that will slowly destroy his mind. He is pragmatic about the news, just wanting to know what he can do to control his symptoms. ReporterSam Milleris tipped off about Kane seeing Dr. Harris and attempts to question her. He gets nothing from her but their meeting is noticed by Kane's staff, who have been tailing Miller. Kane authorizes a gentle warning to be delivered to Dr. Harris through his Chief of StaffEzra Stone.Stone's manuses a neuromuscular blocker to paralyze the doctor as she goes to pick up her son from a little league game and delivers a chilling reminder of her oath, terrifying her.[1]

characters.name: Ella Harris
characters.description: Ella Harrisis a neurologist. She is treating MayorTom Kanefor Lewy Body Dementia. Dr. Harris sees Mayor of ChicagoTom Kanein secrecy, meeting him at a disused abattoir. She diagnoses him with Lewy Body Dementia, a progressive degenerative disease that will slowly destroy his mind. He is pragmatic about the news, just wanting to know what he can do to control his symptoms. ReporterSam Milleris tipped off about Kane seeing Dr. Harris and attempts to question her. He gets nothing from her but their meeting is noticed by Kane's staff, who have been tailing Miller. Kane authorizes a gentle warning to be delivered to Dr. Harris through his Chief of StaffEzra Stone.Stone's manuses a neuromuscular blocker to paralyze the doctor as she goes to pick up her son from a little league game and delivers a chilling reminder of her oath, terrifying her.[1]

characters.name: Max Harris
characters.description: Max Harrisis the son of neurologist Dr.Ella Harris. ReporterSam Milleris tipped off about MayorTom Kaneseeing Dr. Harris and attempts to question her. He gets nothing but their meeting is noticed by Kane's staff, who have been tailing Miller. Kane authorizes a gentle warning to be delivered to Dr. Harris through his Chief of StaffEzra Stone.Stone's manuses a neuromuscular blocker to paralyze the doctor as she goes to pick up Max from a little league game and delivers a chilling reminder of her oath, terrifying her. Max joins her in the car just as she is recovering.[1] Tom Kanearranges to see Dr. Harris in secret at her home. He offers to facilitate a move back to Chicago and to arrange for Max to be enrolled in a private school specializing in children with academic difficulties.[2]

characters.name: Ben Zajac
characters.description: Ben Zajacis the Illinois State Treasurer and a candidate for Governor of Illinois. He is married toMaggie Zajacand they have two sons. Zajac wins the support of MayorTom Kaneto run for Governor of Illinois. Kane is looking to oust his crony GovernorMcCall Cullen. He takes Zajac to the City Hall roof garden, ensuring that their meeting is publicized and sending a message to  Cullen. Zajac attends a Latino Association dinner where Kane receives an award. After the dinner Zajac begins an affair with Kane's key aideKitty O'Neill.[1] Ben Zajac is a major character in thefirstandsecondseasons. He is played by starring cast memberJeff Hephner.

characters.name: Ben Zajac
characters.description: Ben Zajacis the Illinois State Treasurer and a candidate for Governor of Illinois. He is married toMaggie Zajacand they have two sons. Zajac wins the support of MayorTom Kaneto run for Governor of Illinois. Kane is looking to oust his crony GovernorMcCall Cullen. After the dinner Zajac begins an affair with Kane's key aideKitty O'Neill.[1] Ben Zajac is a major character in thefirstandsecondseasons. He is played by starring cast memberJeff Hephner.

characters.name: Kane's Aide
characters.description: Kane's Aideis a staffer in the Mayor's office. He is also called a "phone monkey" by MayorTom Kane. He brings Kane a bottle of water after his meeting with Alderman Mata.[1] Kane's Aide is a recurring character in thefirstandsecondseasons. He is played by co-starSteve Lenzand debuts in "Listen".

characters.name: Kane's Aide
characters.description: Kane's Aideis a staffer in the Mayor's office. He is also called a "phone monkey" by MayorTom Kane. He brings Kane a bottle of water after his meeting with Alderman Mata.[1] Kane's Aide is a recurring character in thefirstandsecondseasons. He is played by co-starSteve Lenzand debuts in "Listen".

characters.name: Kane's Driver
characters.description: Kane's Driveris an aide responsible for transporting MayorTom Kanearound the city. He brings Kane to City Hall from the airport after his out of town meeting with Dr.Ella Harris.[1] Kane's Driver is an unnamed minor character in thesecondseason. He is played by co-starJames Modzelewskiand only appears in "Louder Than Words".

characters.name: Kane's Driver
characters.description: Kane's Driveris an aide responsible for transporting MayorTom Kanearound the city. He brings Kane to City Hall from the airport after his out of town meeting with Dr.Ella Harris.[1] Kane's Driver is an unnamed minor character in thesecondseason. He is played by co-starJames Modzelewskiand only appears in "Louder Than Words".

characters.name: Emma Kane
characters.description: Emma Kaanis the daughter of Chicago MayorTom KaneandMeredith Kane. She is in Season 1, an Episcopalian cleric and a nurse at the church's free medical clinic. In Season 2 she is incarcerated for drug offenses, later commuted to house arrest. Emma's substance abuse issues are a central, recurring theme throughout the course of the show's story line. Emma's father tries to call her. She avoids his calls, engrossing herself in work as a rector at her church and volunteering at a free medical clinic. However, she relapses to substance abuse after meeting a sweet young man involved in drug dealing namedDarius Morrison.[1]Towards the end of Season 1, she is caught up in a sweep of drug busts throughout the city, which Mayor Kane had ordered to help boost his political reputation. She is subsequently jailed and defrocked from her church position, with the free clinic in the church being shut down. Emma maintains she was merely doing what she had to to help poor residents of Chicago get the medical care they needed. Upset with her parents, Emma resists attempts by her father to move her to the hospital to visit her mother, Meredith, after she is shot at O'Hare's International. Her incarceration at a detention center proves harsh, as her fellow inmates show animosity towards her for being the daughter of the mayor. Her sentence is later commuted to house arrest. She struggles with attempts to stay sober while being further and further estranged from Darius, who with time begins to turn aggressive.

characters.name: Emma Kane
characters.description: Emma Kaanis the daughter of Chicago MayorTom KaneandMeredith Kane. She is in Season 1, an Episcopalian cleric and a nurse at the church's free medical clinic. In Season 2 she is incarcerated for drug offenses, later commuted to house arrest. Emma's substance abuse issues are a central, recurring theme throughout the course of the show's story line. Emma's father tries to call her. She avoids his calls, engrossing herself in work as a rector at her church and volunteering at a free medical clinic. However, she relapses to substance abuse after meeting a sweet young man involved in drug dealing namedDarius Morrison.[1]Towards the end of Season 1, she is caught up in a sweep of drug busts throughout the city, which Mayor Kane had ordered to help boost his political reputation. She is subsequently jailed and defrocked from her church position, with the free clinic in the church being shut down. Emma maintains she was merely doing what she had to to help poor residents of Chicago get the medical care they needed. Upset with her parents, Emma resists attempts by her father to move her to the hospital to visit her mother, Meredith, after she is shot at O'Hare's International. Her incarceration at a detention center proves harsh, as her fellow inmates show animosity towards her for being the daughter of the mayor. Her sentence is later commuted to house arrest. She struggles with attempts to stay sober while being further and further estranged from Darius, who with time begins to turn aggressive.

characters.name: Meredith Kane
characters.description: Meredith Kaneis a political power broker from a wealthy and influential family. She is in a loveless marriage and political alliance with MayorTom Kane. They are estranged from their daughterEmma Kane. Meredith attends a campaign event for GovernorMcCall Cullenwhere Tom speaks in support of the Governor publicly. Tom backs away from his crony to support young and ambitious state treasurerBen Zajacto replace him after the event. Meredith controls funds for school improvement with the Mayor's backing and a publicity event goes awry when journalists notice concealed mold at a supposedly much improved inner city school. She warns the corporation responsible to get their act together because the bad publicity reflects poorly on her and the mayor. Meredith later accompanies Kane to a Latino Association dinner where he receives an award. After the dinner Kane has an icy exchange with Meredith and then retires to his (separate) bedroom.[1]

characters.name: Meredith Kane

Meredith later accompanies Kane to a Latino Association dinner where he receives an award. After the dinner Kane has an icy exchange with Meredith and then retires to his (separate) bedroom.[1]

characters.name: Ryan Kavanaugh
characters.description: Ryan Kavanaughis an old friend of MayorTom Kaneand a retired police detective. When Tom Kane is doubting if he is a monster, Ryan Kavanaugh assures him he's a great man because he has built so much for Chicago. When Kane seeks advice, Kavenaugh says that Tom has to do something bad to do something good. Ryan Kavanaugh pursuades Gabriela Reyes to publically redact her statement against Kane.
